title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Understanding ULIP Surrender Value: A Complete Guide
Unit-linked insurance plans (ULIP) are investment-oriented life insurance plans that help you create a market-linked corpus for your financial goals. When buying ULIPs, it is recommended to choose a suitable policy term that aligns with your financial goals. Moreover, when you choose a long-term tenure, you can create a good corpus through compounding of returns. Plus, the market volatilities tend to smooth out, allowing you to earn good returns on the invested premium.
However, you might exit the ULIP policy before the tenure ends. This is called surrender, and when you do so, you get the ULIP surrender value. Let’s understand what this value means, how it is calculated, and how it affects long-term savings.
What is ULIP Surrender Value?
The ULIP surrender value is what you get in case of surrendering your Unit Linked Insurance Plan before the date of maturity. It is simply the exit value of your ULIP, minus applicable charges, as per IRDAI guidelines.
ULIPs have a lock-in period of 5 years, and the surrender value depends on when you exit from the policy – during the lock-in period or after the lock-in period.
Understanding the ULIP surrender value is important as it impacts both your returns and your long-term wealth creation objectives. Knowing how surrendering impacts protection and savings assists you in making wise decisions.
How is the Surrender Value of a ULIP Policy Calculated?
The surrender value in a ULIP policy is determined by deducting any charges that may be applicable from the value of the funds at the time of surrender.
Basic Formula:
Surrender Value = Fund Value – Applicable Charges
The fund value is determined by multiplying the units you hold by the current Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit. There are, however, certain charges in ULIPs that are deducted from the amount received when the policy is surrendered earlier than the maturity period, including premium allocation charges, charges for fund management, surrender charges, etc. The policy term is also relevant.
When the ULIP plan is surrendered in the 5-year lock-in, the value of the fund (after deduction of applicable charges) is moved to a discontinued policy fund and paid upon expiry of the lock-in period. Upon expiry of the lock-in, the surrender value is the same as the fund value with minimal deductions.
The most important factors determining the surrender value are:
NAV (Net Asset Value): Refers to the present value of each unit you possess.
Discontinuation Charges: Charges involved if surrendered before the lock-in.
Policy Duration: The tenure after which you are surrendering the policy. The longer the tenure, the higher the surrender value and vice versa.
When Can You Surrender a ULIP Policy?
The ULIP policy can be surrendered only as per IRDAI rules, where there is a lock-in period of 5 years. The policyholders are not at liberty to withdraw the funds during this period. In case of surrender of policy within 5 years, the fund value is shifted to a Discontinued Policy (DP) fund after deducting discontinuation charges. The returns are paid only after the lock-in period is over, and the returns here are generally low.
But if the surrender is made after 5 years, the full fund value based on the prevailing NAV is paid out to the policyholder without any further deductions. This renders surrender after the lock-in a better alternative.
Though premature surrender enables exit, it is important to know the surrender value so that you know what to expect from the policy:
ULIP Surrender Timeline:
0-5 years: Premature surrender to DP fund, charges deducted, and payment after 5 years.
Post 5 years: Free surrender, entire fund value paid immediately.
Charges and Deductions Impacting ULIP Surrender
When you buy the ULIP plan, premium allocation charges are deducted to meet the issuance cost of the policy. During the policy tenure, fund management charges (capped by IRDAI), mortality charges, administrative charges, etc., are deducted from the fund value.
If you surrender during the lock-in period, the policy discontinuation charge is levied, which further reduces the fund value. All these charges reduce the surrender value, affecting the actual money that you receive on premature exits.
Knowing these deductions allows the policyholders to estimate the real surrender corpus and make proper decisions.
Is ULIP Policy Surrender Amount Taxable?
The surrender value of a ULIP (Unit-Linked Insurance Plan) can be taxed based on time and regulatory ceilings. If the ULIP is surrendered within the first 5 years, the premium deduction available under Section 80C would be reversed, and you would be taxed on the premiums too.
Moreover, if you surrender the policy within the lock-in period, the surrender value would be taxable. The value would be added to your total income and taxed at your slab rates.
If surrendered beyond the 5-year lock-in period, the surrender value would be tax-free under Section 10(10D). However, recent changes have brought in new thresholds:
For ULIPs made on or after February 1, 2021, if the total annual premium paid under ULIPs is more than ₹2.5 lakh, the policy is no longer exempted under Section 10(10D) its surrender or maturity proceeds are now taxed as capital gains with applicable STT and TDS.
How to Check the ULIP Surrender Value Online?
Verifying the surrender value of an ULIP policy online is convenient and fast, and can be achieved in a matter of a few steps:
Log in to your insurer's online portal or app - Enter your credentials to see the current fund or surrender value.
Utilise a ULIP calculator- Provide information like premium paid, policy term, and fund value to receive an estimate.
Check your policy statement or document - Policy details usually outline how surrender value is determined after charges.
Check with customer service if in doubt - If online information appears to be ambiguous, call up your insurer's customer care for correct values.
By doing so, policyholders can simply find out their surrender value without going to a branch, enabling them to make more informed financial decisions.
Important Tips Before Surrendering Your ULIP Policy
Before you decide to surrender your ULIP policy, weigh these points carefully:
Know the Lock-in Period & Charges— ULIPs have a compulsory five-year lock-in. If you withdraw before that, your money goes into a Discontinued Policy (DP) fund, open to discontinuation charges and poorer returns.
Evaluate Financial Impact—Early surrender often results in significant losses due to charges and reduced fund value; staying invested longer may yield better returns.
Understand the Tax Implications—Pre-lock-in surrender could attract reversal of earlier tax advantages (such as Section 80C deductions), TDS deductions, and taxation based on your slab. After the lock-in period, proceeds could be tax-free under certain circumstances.
Loss of Life Cover—Surrender terminates your life insurance coverage. This also stops the life insurance coverage that the policy provided.
So, think through and make an informed decision.
Key Takeaways
ULIP surrender value is the amount of NAV accumulated over the period after deducting fees such as discontinuation, fund management, and mortality charges.
Policies are locked in for 5 years; surrendering before this transfers funds to a DP fund with minimal returns.
Tax exemption under Section 10(10D) if premiums are within limits; otherwise, surrender proceeds can be taxed as income or as capital gains.
Surrender terminates life cover, so think of alternatives before quitting. Surrender only when liquidity is immediate or objectives change.
Conclusion
In short, it's important to know the surrender value of a ULIP before a surrender decision is made. Although ULIPs are meant for long-term wealth creation and life cover, premature surrendering can wear away returns through charges, tax outgo, and the loss of insurance cover. Assessing the five-year lock-in, applicable deductions, and tax provisions under Section 10(10D) prevents policyholders from making impulsive choices. Ultimately, surrendering should be a last resort, undertaken only when financial needs or future financial goals outweigh the long-term benefits of staying invested.
FAQs
What happens if I surrender my ULIP before the 5-year lock-in period?
Your money goes to a Discontinued Policy (DP) fund after deduction of charges; withdrawals are postponed till the lock-in date, earning little interest, and cancelling the tax relief availed in the past.
Can I surrender only a part of my ULIP policy, or do I need to surrender the entire policy?
You cannot surrender in part; surrender is for the whole policy. ULIPs also permit partial withdrawal after lock-in, so some money can be accessed without ending coverage.
How long does it take to receive the surrender value after policy submission?
Most insurers release the surrender value quickly after receiving the final documents. You can check with your insurance company about the tentative timeline for receiving the surrender value.
Will surrendering my ULIP policy affect my life insurance coverage?
Yes, surrendering will terminate your life insurance coverage since you exit the policy.
Are partial withdrawals and surrender value the same?
No. Partial withdrawals enable you to withdraw a part of the fund value after lock-in, but with the policy remaining active. Surrender value is given on fully closing the policy.
Can NRIs surrender their ULIP policies in India?
Yes, NRIs can surrender ULIPs bought in India at prevailing charges, tax, and FEMA regulations. Proceeds are repatriable following policy conditions and regulatory approvals.
